commands.meaning: Enters global configuration mode on a Cisco IOS device.
use_for: Start an approved configuration change
Prepare to edit interfaces, VLANs, routing, or device settings
good_output: No authorization or invalid input error

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The device did not accept global configuration mode.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The account may not have privilege, or the command may be unsupported in the current mode.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not attempt changes with another account unless the change is approved and logged.

next_commands.why: Collects current active configuration before changes.
next_commands.meaning: Displays the active configuration in memory.

commands.safety_level: High - enters configuration mode; later commands can affect production.
commands.learning_note: Configuration mode is not dangerous by itself, but it is the doorway to state-changing commands.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Enters configuration mode for a specific Cisco interface.
use_for: Prepare to change a port
Apply description, VLAN, shutdown, or speed/duplex settings

next_commands.why: Checks current interface settings before making changes.
next_commands.meaning: Shows the active configuration for one interface.
look_for_good: Expected description, VLAN, and shutdown state

commands.safety_level: High - selects a production interface for possible changes.
commands.learning_note: Always confirm the exact interface from read-only output before entering interface configuration.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Administratively disables the selected interface.
use_for: Disable an approved unused or faulty port
Stop traffic during an approved change
bad_output: Critical uplink or AP port shut

next_commands.why: Confirms which port changed state.

commands.safety_level: Critical - can disconnect users, APs, cameras, uplinks, or services.
commands.learning_note: Shutdown is reversible, but the outage is immediate if the wrong interface is selected.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Administratively enables the selected interface.
use_for: Bring an approved port back into service
Recover a port that was intentionally disabled
good_output: Interface is no longer administratively down
Link comes up only when expected

next_commands.why: Checks link and error counters after enabling the port.
next_commands.meaning: Shows detailed interface state and counters.

commands.safety_level: High - can restore traffic or introduce an unexpected device to the network.
commands.learning_note: No shutdown should follow ownership, VLAN, and physical endpoint confirmation.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Assigns the selected access port to a specific VLAN.
use_for: Move an approved endpoint to the correct VLAN
Fix an access port with the wrong VLAN
good_output: Endpoint gets expected network after reconnect or DHCP renewal

next_commands.why: Confirms the VLAN exists and the port appears in the expected VLAN.

commands.safety_level: High - changes endpoint network placement and can break access.
commands.learning_note: VLAN changes affect IP assignment, ACLs, voice services, and reachability.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Saves the active running configuration to startup configuration.
use_for: Persist an approved change across reload
Save the final known-good configuration

next_commands.why: Confirms the saved configuration is present.
next_commands.meaning: Displays the configuration that loads after reboot.

commands.safety_level: Medium - persists current changes; can preserve a mistake across reboot.
commands.learning_note: Save only after validating the active change and confirming no unrelated pending changes are present.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Restarts the Cisco device.
use_for: Perform an approved reboot
Complete maintenance that requires reload
good_output: Reload occurs during an approved window

next_commands.why: Checks uptime and software after the device returns.

commands.safety_level: Critical - reboots the device and interrupts users.
commands.learning_note: Reload should require approval, change window, config save decision, and rollback plan.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Enters Comware system configuration view.
use_for: Start an approved Comware configuration change
Prepare to edit interfaces, VLANs, IRF, or system settings

next_commands.why: Collects current configuration before changes.

commands.safety_level: High - enters configuration mode; later commands can affect production.
commands.learning_note: System view is the Comware doorway to state-changing commands.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Enters Comware interface view for a selected interface.
use_for: Prepare to change a port
Apply shutdown, VLAN, description, or link settings

next_commands.why: Checks current interface settings before making changes.
next_commands.meaning: Shows active configuration for one interface.
look_for_good: Expected VLAN, shutdown state, and description

commands.safety_level: High - selects a production interface for possible changes.
commands.learning_note: Confirm the interface from read-only output before entering interface view.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Administratively disables the selected Comware interface.
use_for: Disable an approved unused or faulty port
Stop traffic during an approved change
good_output: Interface shows ADM or administratively down

next_commands.why: Confirms which interface changed state.

commands.safety_level: Critical - can disconnect users, APs, cameras, uplinks, or stack links.
commands.learning_note: On Comware, always verify you are not in an IRF or uplink interface before shutdown.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Administratively enables the selected Comware interface.
use_for: Bring an approved port back into service
Recover an interface that was administratively disabled

next_commands.why: Checks detailed state after enabling the port.

commands.safety_level: High - can restore traffic or introduce an unexpected device to the network.
commands.learning_note: Undo shutdown should follow endpoint ownership, VLAN, and physical confirmation.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Assigns the selected Comware access port to a VLAN.
use_for: Move an approved endpoint to the correct VLAN
Correct wrong access VLAN on a port

next_commands.why: Confirms VLAN membership after the change.

commands.safety_level: High - changes endpoint network placement and can break access.
commands.learning_note: VLAN changes can affect DHCP, gateway, security policy, and voice/data separation.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Saves the current Comware configuration so it persists after reboot.
use_for: Persist an approved change
Save a validated known-good configuration

next_commands.why: Confirms the saved configuration.
next_commands.meaning: Shows the configuration saved for next boot.

commands.safety_level: Medium - persists current changes; can preserve mistakes across reboot.
commands.learning_note: Save after validating service impact and checking for unrelated pending changes.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Restarts the Comware device or member.
use_for: Perform an approved reboot
Complete maintenance requiring restart
good_output: Device or member returns as expected

next_commands.why: Checks uptime and software after reboot.

commands.safety_level: Critical - reboots equipment and interrupts users.
commands.learning_note: Reboot needs approval, save decision, maintenance window, and rollback plan.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Enters global configuration mode on ArubaOS-CX.
use_for: Start an approved Aruba configuration change
Prepare to edit interfaces, VLANs, routing, or system settings

next_commands.why: Collects current configuration before changes.

commands.safety_level: High - enters configuration mode; later commands can affect production.
commands.learning_note: Configuration mode is the starting point for state-changing ArubaOS-CX commands.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Enters ArubaOS-CX configuration mode for a specific interface.
use_for: Prepare to change a port
Apply description, VLAN, shutdown, or link settings

next_commands.why: Checks current interface settings before change.

commands.safety_level: High - selects a production interface for possible changes.
commands.learning_note: Confirm interface identity before entering config context.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Administratively disables the selected ArubaOS-CX interface.
use_for: Disable an approved unused or faulty port
Stop traffic during an approved change

next_commands.why: Confirms which interface changed state.

commands.safety_level: Critical - can disconnect users, APs, cameras, uplinks, or services.
commands.learning_note: Shutdown is immediate; verify the interface and endpoint before use.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Administratively enables the selected ArubaOS-CX interface.
use_for: Bring an approved interface back into service
Recover a port that was intentionally disabled
good_output: Interface is no longer admin down

next_commands.why: Checks detailed state after enabling the port.

commands.safety_level: High - can restore traffic or introduce an unexpected device to the network.
commands.learning_note: No shutdown should follow endpoint, VLAN, and ownership confirmation.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Sets the access VLAN for the selected ArubaOS-CX interface.
use_for: Move an approved endpoint to the correct VLAN
Correct wrong access VLAN

next_commands.why: Confirms VLAN exists and membership is expected.

commands.safety_level: High - changes endpoint network placement and can break access.
commands.learning_note: Access VLAN changes affect IP assignment and policy reachability.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Saves the active ArubaOS-CX running configuration.
use_for: Persist an approved change
Save validated configuration

next_commands.why: Confirms saved configuration.

commands.safety_level: Medium - persists current changes; can preserve a mistake across reboot.
commands.learning_note: Save only after validating the intended change and checking for unrelated pending changes.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Restarts the ArubaOS-CX device.
use_for: Perform an approved reboot
Complete maintenance requiring restart

next_commands.why: Checks uptime and image after reload.

commands.safety_level: Critical - reboots the device and interrupts users.
commands.learning_note: Reload needs approval, maintenance window, config save decision, and rollback plan.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify platform syntax and change procedure before use.

commands.meaning: Releases the DHCP lease from Windows network adapters.
use_for: Force DHCP renewal troubleshooting
Clear a stale DHCP lease before renew
bad_output: Adapter has no valid IP until renewed

next_commands.why: Requests a new DHCP lease after release.

commands.safety_level: Medium - can disconnect the endpoint until DHCP renews.
commands.learning_note: Avoid releasing DHCP over a remote session unless you have out-of-band access.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify syntax and local privilege before use.

commands.meaning: Requests a new DHCP lease for Windows network adapters.
use_for: Recover from DHCP or APIPA state
Refresh IP settings after VLAN or DHCP changes

next_commands.why: Checks full adapter settings after renewal.

commands.safety_level: Medium - can briefly interrupt endpoint connectivity.
commands.learning_note: Renew is useful after DHCP or VLAN fixes, but evidence should confirm the adapter and network first.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify syntax and local privilege before use.

commands.command: netsh interface ipv4 set address name=<name> static <ip> <mask> <gateway>
commands.meaning: Configures a static IPv4 address on a Windows interface.
use_for: Set approved static addressing
Recover a device that must use fixed IP settings
good_output: ipconfig shows intended IP, mask, and gateway

next_commands.why: Verifies the static configuration.
look_for_good: Expected static IP, mask, gateway, DNS

commands.safety_level: High - changes endpoint addressing and can break remote access.
commands.learning_note: Static IP changes should be made only with exact interface name, IP plan, and rollback path.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify syntax and local privilege before use.

commands.meaning: Disables a Windows network interface.
use_for: Disable an approved adapter
Troubleshoot adapter failover or duplicate connections

next_commands.why: Confirms adapter admin state.
next_commands.meaning: Shows Windows interface admin and connect state.

commands.safety_level: Critical - can disconnect the endpoint or remote session.
commands.learning_note: Never disable the only management adapter during a remote session without alternate access.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify syntax and local privilege before use.

commands.meaning: Enables a Windows network interface.
use_for: Bring an approved adapter back into service
Recover a disabled adapter

next_commands.why: Checks addressing after enabling the adapter.

commands.safety_level: High - can change active network path and endpoint reachability.
commands.learning_note: Confirm interface name and intended network before enabling adapters.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify syntax and local privilege before use.

commands.meaning: Resets the Windows Winsock catalog.
use_for: Repair suspected Windows socket stack corruption
Recover after broken network software changes

next_commands.why: Checks adapter state after reboot or reset workflow.

commands.safety_level: High - changes local network stack and usually requires reboot.
commands.learning_note: Use Winsock reset after evidence points to local stack issues, not as the first troubleshooting step.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify syntax and local privilege before use.

commands.meaning: Administratively brings a Linux network interface down.
use_for: Disable an approved interface
Force link reset during an approved local change

next_commands.why: Confirms interface state after the action.
next_commands.meaning: Shows Linux interface state and IP addresses.

commands.safety_level: Critical - can disconnect SSH, services, or production traffic.
commands.learning_note: Avoid bringing down the management interface unless you have console or alternate access.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify distro/tool behavior before use.

commands.meaning: Administratively brings a Linux network interface up.
use_for: Enable an approved interface
Recover an interface that was administratively down
good_output: Interface state becomes UP or LOWER_UP when connected

next_commands.why: Checks routing after enabling the interface.

commands.safety_level: High - can change active network path and service reachability.
commands.learning_note: Interface up does not guarantee IP or routing; verify state, address, and route.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify distro/tool behavior before use.

commands.meaning: Releases a DHCP lease on a Linux interface using dhclient.
use_for: Clear stale DHCP lease
Prepare for a fresh DHCP request

next_commands.why: Requests a new DHCP lease after release.

commands.safety_level: Medium - can remove IP connectivity until renewed.
commands.learning_note: DHCP release is risky over SSH if it targets the active management interface.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify distro/tool behavior before use.

commands.meaning: Requests a DHCP lease on a Linux interface using dhclient.
use_for: Recover missing IP assignment
Refresh DHCP after VLAN or DHCP changes

next_commands.why: Checks assigned address after DHCP.

commands.safety_level: Medium - can change endpoint IP settings.
commands.learning_note: Verify address, route, and DNS after DHCP renewal.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify distro/tool behavior before use.

commands.meaning: Restarts the Linux NetworkManager service.
use_for: Recover NetworkManager-managed connectivity
Apply approved local network service changes
good_output: Interfaces return with expected IP settings

next_commands.why: Checks service health after restart.

commands.safety_level: High - can briefly or permanently interrupt Linux network connectivity.
commands.learning_note: Restarting network management services can drop SSH; use console or approved remote path.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify distro/tool behavior before use.

commands.meaning: Restarts the traditional Linux networking service on distributions that use it.
use_for: Apply approved network config changes
Recover service-managed connectivity

next_commands.why: Checks default route after networking restart.

commands.safety_level: High - can interrupt Linux network connectivity.
commands.learning_note: Some distributions use NetworkManager or systemd-networkd instead of networking.service.
commands.source_note: Local admin command knowledge base. Verify distro/tool behavior before use.
